note_evidence: 21-year-old female, G1, presenting for her first prenatal visit ... By her last menstrual period (mid-January) she estimates gestational age at approximately 9–10 weeks. This is her first pregnancy.
rationale: Age, gravidity, gestational age, and LMP all documented.

note_evidence: She reports a missed period followed by two home pregnancy tests that were positive approximately two weeks ago.
In-office pregnancy test: Positive (confirmed today)
She reports significant anxiety related to the pregnancy.
rationale: Anxiety about pregnancy documented; everyday stress also addressed in plan.

note_evidence: Chronic neck and low back pain following a fall from a horse (barrel racing) approximately two years ago. Pain severity fluctuates from mild to debilitating.
rationale: Location, cause, onset, and severity fluctuation documented.

note_evidence: Hydrocodone/acetaminophen 5 mg — for severe pain days ... taking these "most days" recently
rationale: Drug, dose, indication, and frequency documented.

note_evidence: Tramadol 50 mg — for moderate pain days
do NOT stop abruptly (withdrawal risk to patient and fetus discussed)
rationale: Counseling on withdrawal risk to mother and fetus documented.

note_evidence: Continue current medications at the lowest functional dose ... Provider to contact pain specialist this week to collaboratively develop a gradual, written taper schedule.
rationale: Gradual taper with specialist and continuing current dose documented.

note_evidence: Counseled on risk of neonatal withdrawal with continued opioid use and rationale for taper.
Provider to contact pain specialist this week to collaboratively develop a gradual, written taper schedule.
rationale: Coordination with pain specialist this week documented.

note_evidence: pregnancy-safe non-pharmacologic pain strategies: physical therapy, heat, stretching, activity pacing, and positioning adjustments
Bleeding/Spotting: None
No significant cramping; occasional low, mild pulling "twinges"
Significant nausea, predominantly mornings, with several episodes of vomiting
Urinary: Increased frequency; no dysuria ... Fevers: None
rationale: No dysuria and no fevers documented.

note_evidence: Urinary: Increased frequency; no dysuria
Only her baseline neck-related headaches; no new headaches or visual changes
No smoking history; One glass of wine at a birthday prior to knowing she was pregnant; none since; Denies other substance use
rationale: Tobacco, alcohol, and other substance history documented.

note_evidence: Discontinue horseback riding (fall risk) for the duration of pregnancy. Ground activities with horses permitted.
Recently completed her degree; not currently working due to chronic pain.
Intrauterine pregnancy confirmed; Cardiac activity present (fetal heartbeat visualized); Size consistent with stated dates; Impression: Normal, healthy early intrauterine pregnancy
